mission.title: Install and Configure Clusterpedia on Kubernetes
mission.description: Clusterpedia is an encyclopedia of Kubernetes clusters that allows for synchronization, searching, and management of multi-cluster resources. Installing Clusterpedia enables efficient control and visibility over your Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Clusterpedia Helm repository to your local Helm client to access the charts.
```bash
helm repo add clusterpedia https://clusterpedia-io.github.io/clusterpedia-helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Clusterpedia using Helm
steps.description:
Install Clusterpedia in the 'clusterpedia-system' namespace. The `installCRDs=true` flag is required to install the Custom Resource Definitions.
```bash
helm install clusterpedia clusterpedia/clusterpedia --namespace clusterpedia-system --create-namespace --version 2.6.2 --set installCRDs=true
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Clusterpedia pods are running correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace clusterpedia-system
```

steps.title: Verify CRDs and API aggregation
steps.description:
Confirm that the Clusterpedia CRDs and aggregated API service are registered:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep clusterpedia.io
kubectl get apiservice | grep clusterpedia
```
You should see pediaclusters, clustersyncresources, clusterimportpolicies, and pediaclusterlifecycles CRDs.

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Clusterpedia release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall clusterpedia --namespace clusterpedia-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove all Custom Resource Definitions associated with Clusterpedia:
```bash
kubectl get crd -o name | grep clusterpedia.io | xargs kubectl delete
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'clusterpedia-system' namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace clusterpedia-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of Clusterpedia resources to ensure you can restore if needed.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace clusterpedia-system -o yaml > clusterpedia-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to fetch the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Clusterpedia
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Clusterpedia installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade clusterpedia clusterpedia/clusterpedia --namespace clusterpedia-system --version 2.6.2
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Clusterpedia pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace clusterpedia-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the Clusterpedia pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace clusterpedia-system
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: API server not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Clusterpedia API server, ensure the service is running and the port-forward command is active.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace clusterpedia-system
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If a pod is in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace clusterpedia-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Clusterpedia will have all pods running in the 'clusterpedia-system' namespace, and you will be able to access the API server through port forwarding. Resource limits should be set for optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add clusterpedia https://clusterpedia-io.github.io/clusterpedia-helm
helm repo update
helm install clusterpedia clusterpedia/clusterpedia --namespace clusterpedia-system --create-namespace --version 2.6.2 --set installCRDs=true

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
